summary: Big Investo is only enterprise-ready when live proof, repo hygiene, product UX, tenant isolation, mail delivery, identity, billing, workflows, and operations pass together. This audit intentionally blocks readiness on any open P0/P1 full-stack gap.

domains.name: Market and FDE operating model
keep: Real buyer/agency workflows and role journeys as the product spine.
remove: Vague readiness claims and docs-only readiness.
upgrade: Add per-ICP market proof: paid pilot, onboarding time, first-value time, retained usage, and support burden.

checks.action: Keep the ICP, journey, and buyer proof docs current.
Maintain it as the source for live proof.
Do not sell as enterprise-ready until all gates pass live.

domains.name: Frontend UX, role paths, and workflow coverage
keep: Role-specific flows and invite journey E2E coverage.
remove: Decorative or marketing-first screens that hide the actual CRM workflow.
upgrade: Add Playwright suites for platform admin no-tenant state, tenant-selected state, company admin, sales agent, operations, viewer, mobile.

checks.action: Keep buildable Vite app.
Add role-by-role and mobile visual proofs for admin/staff/viewer flows.
Keep invite onboarding under E2E.

domains.name: Backend domain integrity and tenant isolation
keep: Company-scoped APIs, explicit super-admin target context, and production role proof.
remove: Implicit tenant scoping and generic 500s in business workflows.
upgrade: Expand matrix from users into leads, analytics, conversations, visits, billing, notifications, and property imports.

checks.action: Keep backend build and smoke gates.
Keep expanding contract and isolation tests.

checks.message: Tenant isolation gate is passing in current report.
checks.action: Block release on any tenant isolation regression.

checks.message: Role matrix gate is passing in current report.
checks.action: Block release on role matrix regression.

domains.name: AI, WhatsApp, and workflow reliability
keep: Single-reply contract, takeover-safe behavior, and smoke-tested buyer scenarios.
remove: Manual-only WhatsApp confidence and hidden delivery failures.
upgrade: Add queue/worker proofs, idempotency dashboards, and per-tenant WhatsApp credential isolation evidence.

checks.action: Run with real device evidence before pilot claims.
Keep buyer/staff WhatsApp proof separate from synthetic tests.
Run it before every release.

domains.name: Mail, onboarding, and invite delivery
keep: Accepted-send tracking, webhook ingestion, resend/retry UI.
remove: UI copy that says email sent when provider did not accept the message.
upgrade: Finish delivery-event proof: delivered, bounced, delayed, suppressed, failed, resend retry.

checks.message: Strict mail delivery gate is not passing.
checks.action: Configure Resend webhook secret or read-capable audit key, then rerun proof.
Keep invite create/accept/resend in production proof.
Register the production webhook and validate Svix signatures live.

blockers: Strict mail delivery gate is not passing.

domains.name: Identity, security, and compliance
keep: Keycloak SSO, MFA, SCIM, DR, incident, and on-call runbooks.
remove: Production test SSO callback paths and any tokenized secrets in code/remotes.
upgrade: Add security regression tests for IP allowlist, DSR, audit logs, token rotation, and incident drills.

checks.message: Enterprise identity gate is passing in current report.
checks.action: Keep SSO/MFA/SCIM proof live.
Do not remove SSO routes while FEATURE_SSO=true.
Keep MFA route order behind JSON parser.
Keep SCIM externalId lookup safe.

checks.message: Incident, DR, and on-call runbooks exist.
checks.action: Update with latest Railway/Vercel/Keycloak/Resend operating steps.

domains.name: Billing and commercial operations
keep: Agency invite billing model and platform admin billing surface.
remove: Manual spreadsheet billing as the operating system.
upgrade: Add live proof for subscription state transitions, invoices, payment failures, and tenant suspension UX.

checks.message: No explicit billing failed gate in current report.
checks.action: Add billing proof instead of relying on absence of failure.
Prove billing status, past due, suspend/reactivate, and invoice flows.
Add billing UI E2E coverage and empty/error states.

domains.name: Observability, operations, and reliability
keep: Smoke, synthetic monitor, health endpoints, and on-call docs.
remove: Readiness reports that drift from live deployments.
upgrade: Add Grafana/status-page/on-call proof, RTO/RPO drills, and SLO alert evidence.

checks.action: Keep smoke as a release gate.
Run on schedule and alert on failure.
Back it with live schedule and escalation proof.

checks.message: Exit gate report is fresh within 7 days.
checks.action: Regenerate evidence after every runtime or env change.

domains.name: Repository and deploy hygiene
keep: Source, migrations, tests, docs, and environment examples.
remove: Tracked .env, build outputs, node_modules, caches, tokenized remotes, and stale readiness artifacts.
upgrade: Add CI job for this audit, dependency install from lockfiles, and clean deploy source checks.

checks.action: Run npm run repo:untrack-generated -- --apply in a git-writable environment, then commit the removals.
Do not commit generated artifacts; run npm run repo:untrack-generated -- --apply and keep local artifacts ignored.
Use clean remotes only.
Rotate leaked credentials and purge tracked secrets.

checks.message: CHUNK_STATUS is not older than EXIT_GATE_REPORT.
checks.action: Regenerate or deprecate stale chunk readiness artifacts.

blockers.action: Do not sell as enterprise-ready until all gates pass live.

blockers.message: Strict mail delivery gate is not passing.
blockers.action: Configure Resend webhook secret or read-capable audit key, then rerun proof.
Run npm run repo:untrack-generated -- --apply in a git-writable environment, then commit the removals.
Do not commit generated artifacts; run npm run repo:untrack-generated -- --apply and keep local artifacts ignored.

next_moves.action: Do not sell as enterprise-ready until all gates pass live.
Configure Resend webhook secret or read-capable audit key, then rerun proof.
Run npm run repo:untrack-generated -- --apply in a git-writable environment, then commit the removals.
Do not commit generated artifacts; run npm run repo:untrack-generated -- --apply and keep local artifacts ignored.
